findings.title: Primary fix is correct: src_tbl compared against pre-lowercased set
findings.why_it_matters: Before the fix, _lookup() called src.lower() to produce src_tbl, then tested that lowercase string against `temp_views` which retains original case. Any temp view created with uppercase or mixed-case identifiers (common in Databricks notebooks) would fail the membership test, so _lookup() returned None for every such column. Those phantom temp-view source nodes were never short-circuited, corrupting the final lineage graph. The one-line change to `temp_views_lower` fixes the membership check to match the already-lowercased src_tbl.
findings.suggested_fix: No further change needed — the fix is correct as written.
evidence: Line 735: `src = src.lower()` — src_tbl is derived from the lowercased src, so it is always lowercase.
Line 717: `temp_views_lower = {v.lower() for v in temp_views}` — this is the correct set to test against.
Original line tested `temp_views` (original-case), causing mismatch for any uppercase/mixed-case view name.
Iterative chain-resolution loop at lines 771 also uses `temp_views_lower`, so it was already consistent; only _lookup was inconsistent.

findings.title: Wildcard per-column expansion compares tv_key_tbl to src_tbl.lower() redundantly — but is not broken
findings.why_it_matters: At line 801, `tv_key_tbl == src_tbl.lower()` double-lowercases src_tbl (it is already lowercase at this point because line 786 does `.lower()` before the rsplit). The double `.lower()` is harmless, but it shows the inconsistency that led to the original bug — worth noting for hygiene.
findings.suggested_fix: Change `src_tbl.lower()` to just `src_tbl` at line 801, since src_tbl is already lowercase after line 786's `.lower().rsplit()`.
evidence: Line 786: `src_tbl = e.source_col.lower().rsplit('.', 1)[0]` — src_tbl is definitively lowercase here.
Line 801: `tv_key_tbl == src_tbl.lower()` — redundant second lower(), not a bug but misleading.

findings.title: Test does not verify chained uppercase temp views (chain of two mixed-case views)
findings.why_it_matters: The regression test covers a single-hop uppercase temp view (MY_STAGING → final). The iterative chain-resolution loop at lines 765-782 also uses temp_views_lower for membership; if it had a similar latent bug (it doesn't currently), a chain test would catch it. More importantly, a two-hop chain (RAW_STAGE → MID_STAGE → final, all uppercase) would expose whether the chain loop degrades when intermediate uppercase views are involved. Without this test, a future refactor could reintroduce the bug in the chain path without failing CI.
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a test with two chained uppercase temp views:

```python
def test_chained_uppercase_temp_views():
    sql = "\n".join([
        "-- COMMAND ----------",
        "CREATE OR REPLACE TEMPORARY VIEW RAW_STAGE AS",
        "SELECT id FROM uc_dev.raw.src",
        "-- COMMAND ----------",
        "CREATE OR REPLACE TEMPORARY VIEW MID_STAGE AS",
        "SELECT id FROM RAW_STAGE",
        "-- COMMAND ----------",
        "INSERT INTO uc_dev.gold.final SELECT id FROM MID_STAGE",
    ])
    edges = parse_sql(sql, source_file="nb.sql", source_line=1)
    sources = {e.source_col.lower() for e in edges}
    assert "uc_dev.raw.src.id" in sources
    assert not any("raw_stage" in s or "mid_stage" in s for s in sources)
```
evidence: The new test only covers single-hop uppercase view resolution.
The chain loop at lines 765-782 does use temp_views_lower correctly, but that code path is not exercised by the added test.
Chain resolution is identified as a key invariant in the codebase CLAUDE.md.

residual_risks: The fix is scoped to _resolve_temp_views. If other call sites pass temp view names to a different membership check without lowercasing first, the same class of bug could recur. A global audit of `in temp_views` (original set) vs `in temp_views_lower` across the file is advisable.

The chain-resolution loop caps at `len(temp_views_lower) + 1` iterations. If a cycle is inadvertently introduced in temp view references, the loop exits without error and silently produces incomplete lineage. No cycle detection or warning is emitted.
testing_gaps: No test for chained (2+ hop) uppercase temp views through the iterative resolution loop.
No test for a mixed-case temp view name where only the wildcard edge (tbl.*) was recorded, exercising the wildcard-fallback branch of _lookup() with a non-lowercase view name.
No test verifying that a temp view referenced only in a wildcard SELECT * context resolves correctly when the view name is uppercase.
